creator: nicolas.masse@stormshield.eu
text:
Same thing here.
Seems it comes from commit https://reviews.freebsd.org/rS358572.
My analysis is as follow:

When calling ip6_output, rip6_send does not pass the cached route (but why?), which makes ip6_output using the new route KPI. The thing is that new KPI does not take the ipv6 next-hop option into account. As a result, packets are emitted on the wrong interface.

I was able to workaround this issue by passing the cached route to ip6_output in rip6_send. This makes the old KPI being used, making the packets emitted on the right interface.
But the fact is that it does not solve the root cause of the issue, so I have no idea if this is an acceptable solution.
